questions.questionText: Cerebrospinal fluid is produced primarily by:
questions.explanation: Choroid plexus in the ventricles produces CSF.

questions.questionText: Broca's area is located in the:
questions.explanation: Broca's motor speech area is in the inferior frontal gyrus of the dominant hemisphere.

questions.questionText: The primary visual cortex is located in the:
questions.explanation: Area 17 is located in the occipital lobe around the calcarine fissure.

questions.questionText: Wernicke's aphasia results from damage to the:
questions.explanation: Wernicke's sensory speech area is in the posterior part of the superior temporal gyrus.

questions.questionText: The decussation of the pyramids occurs in the:
questions.explanation: Motor fibers cross at the pyramidal decussation in the lower medulla.

questions.questionText: Which cranial nerve emerges from the dorsal aspect of the brainstem?
questions.explanation: Only the trochlear nerve emerges from the dorsal surface of the midbrain.

questions.questionText: The major relay station for sensory information to the cerebral cortex is:
questions.explanation: The thalamus relays all sensory modalities except olfaction to the cortex.

questions.questionText: Parkinson's disease is associated with degeneration of neurons in the:
questions.explanation: Dopaminergic depletion from pars compacta of substantia nigra causes Parkinson's.

questions.questionText: The artery usually involved in an epidural hematoma is the:
questions.explanation: Rupture of MMA at the pterion causes epidural hematoma.

questions.questionText: The representation of the lower limb in the motor homunculus is supplied by:
questions.explanation: ACA supplies the medial surface where lower limb represents.

questions.questionText: In Neuroanatomy, which statement is most accurate regarding the core definition and diagnostic concept?
questions.optionA: It reflects the most exam-relevant definition used in standard Anatomy texts
questions.optionB: It is an obsolete definition no longer used in clinical practice
questions.optionC: It is used only in pediatric population and never in adults
questions.optionD: It has no diagnostic significance in NEET-PG pattern questions
questions.explanation: This tests foundational definition-level knowledge from standard Anatomy references for Neuroanatomy. Subject: Anatomy. Chapter: Neuroanatomy.

questions.questionText: The most important etiology/risk factor to suspect first in a typical Neuroanatomy scenario is:
questions.optionA: The common high-yield risk factor repeatedly asked in entrance exams
questions.optionB: A rare cause seen only in isolated case reports
questions.optionD: An etiology excluded by routine first-line workup
questions.explanation: NEET-PG and INI-CET focus on common, high-yield causes before rare differentials. Subject: Anatomy. Chapter: Neuroanatomy.
